title: Elder Samiddhatta: “The five aggregates are fully understood, Their root is cut off. The wandering of birth is ended, Now there is no further becoming.”
content:
Elder Samiddhatta:
“The five aggregates are fully understood,
Their root is cut off.
The wandering of birth is ended,
Now there is no further becoming.”

title: “Carpenter, what are wholesome thoughts? Renunciation thought, non-ill-will thought, harmlessness thought. These, Carpenter, are called wholesome thoughts.”
content: “Carpenter, what are wholesome thoughts? Renunciation thought, non-ill-will thought, harmlessness thought. These, Carpenter, are called wholesome thoughts.”

title: Sitting down to one side, that monk said to the Venerable Sāriputta: “Venerable Sāriputta, a monk who is my companion has abandoned the training and…
content: Sitting down to one side, that monk said to the Venerable Sāriputta: “Venerable Sāriputta, a monk who is my companion has abandoned the training and returned to the lower life.” - “That comes to be, friend, for one who does not guard the sense faculties, does not know moderation in eating, and is not committed to wakefulness. For such a person, it is impossible that he would continue the perfectly pure holy life for his whole life. Friend, a monk who does guard the sense faculties, knows moderation in eating, and is committed to wakefulness — it is possible that he would continue the perfectly pure holy life for his whole life. Therefore, friend, you should train thus: ‘We will guard the sense faculties, know moderation in eating, and be committed to wakefulness.’ Thus, friend, you should train.”

title: “Monks, it is because of not realizing, not penetrating four things that you and I have wandered for such a long time in this cycle…
content: “Monks, it is because of not realizing, not penetrating four things that you and I have wandered for such a long time in this cycle of birth and death. What four? Monks, because of not realizing, not penetrating noble virtue, you and I have wandered for such a long time in this cycle of birth and death. Monks, because of not realizing, not penetrating noble concentration … noble wisdom … noble liberation, you and I have wandered for such a long time in this cycle of birth and death.”

title: “Monks, form is suffering. What is suffering is not-self. What is not-self should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘This is not…
content: “Monks, form is suffering. What is suffering is not-self. What is not-self should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’ Feeling … Perception … Formations … Consciousness is suffering. What is suffering is not-self. What is not-self should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’ Seeing thus, monks, a well-taught noble disciple becomes disenchanted with form … disenchanted with feeling … disenchanted with perception … disenchanted with formations … disenchanted with consciousness. Being disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion, he is liberated … ‘… there is no more of this state of being.’”

title: “And how, Mahānāma, does a noble disciple guard the sense faculties? Here, Mahānāma, when a noble disciple sees a form with the eye, he does…
content: “And how, Mahānāma, does a noble disciple guard the sense faculties? Here, Mahānāma, when a noble disciple sees a form with the eye, he does not grasp the general feature, does not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the eye faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise—he restrains that cause, guards the eye, practices restraint of the eye. When the ear hears a sound … nose smells an odor … tongue tastes a flavor … body feels a touch … mind cognizes phenomena, he does not grasp the general feature, does not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the mind faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise—he restrains that cause, guards the mind, practices restraint of the mind. Thus, Mahānāma, a noble disciple guards the sense faculties.”

title: “Monks, what is Right Effort? Monks, here a monk awakens desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy,…
content: “Monks, what is Right Effort? Monks, here a monk awakens desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He awakens desire for the abandoning of arisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He awakens desire for the arising of unarisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He awakens desire for the continuance, non-disappearance, strengthening, increase, and fulfillment of arisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. This, monks, is called Right Effort.”

title: Like a great rocky mountain, Standing up to the sky, Advancing and surrounding, Crushing everything on all sides — So too, aging and death Advance…
content:
Like a great rocky mountain,
Standing up to the sky,
Advancing and surrounding,
Crushing everything on all sides —
So too, aging and death
Advance upon beings.
Warriors, brahmins, merchants,
Workers, outcasts,
Those who collect rubbish and feces —
No one can escape.
All are conquered.
No elephant corps,
No chariot corps, no infantry,
No battle of spells,
No battle of wealth
Can help one win
Against aging and death.
Therefore, the wise person,
Seeing what is good for oneself,
Places faith
In the Buddha, Dhamma, and Saṅgha.
Whoever by body, speech, and mind
Practices in accordance with the Dhamma,
Is praised in this life
And in the next enjoys heavenly bliss.

title: “Along with this suffering, abandon sensual desires, Seeking future security from bondage. Rightly understanding, with mind well liberated, There, right there, one is liberated from…
content:
“Along with this suffering, abandon sensual desires,
Seeking future security from bondage.
Rightly understanding, with mind well liberated,
There, right there, one is liberated from contact.
That one attains knowledge,
Accomplishes the holy life,
Reaches the end of the world,
Gets to the far shore —
That one is so called.”

title: “Monks, whatever is impermanent, you should abandon your desire for it. And what, monks, is impermanent? Form, monks, is impermanent … Feeling … Perception ……
content: “Monks, whatever is impermanent, you should abandon your desire for it. And what, monks, is impermanent? Form, monks, is impermanent … Feeling … Perception … Formations … Consciousness is impermanent — there you should abandon your desire. Whatever is impermanent, monks, there you should abandon your desire.”
